workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} and generate all documents in {document_output_language}
🔥 CRITICAL MISSION: Create BMAD story with td integration for maximum observability! 🔥
This workflow EXTENDS the BMM create-story workflow with td sync capabilities
td provides state/observability, BMAD provides structured planning artifacts

step.goal: Initialize td session and determine target story
step.action: Run `td usage --new-session` to initialize td context

check.if: {{story_path}} is provided by user or user provided the epic and story number
check.action: Parse user-provided story path: extract epic_num, story_num, story_title

step.action: Check if {{sprint_status}} file exists for auto discover

check.if: sprint status file does NOT exist
check.output: 🚫 No sprint status file found and no story specified
**Required Options:** 1. Run `sprint-planning` to initialize sprint tracking 2. Provide specific epic-story number (e.g., "1-2-user-auth") 3. Provide path to story documents
check.ask: Choose option [1], provide epic-story number, path, or [q] to quit:

step.action: Load {{sprint_status}} file completely
Find FIRST story with status "backlog" matching pattern: number-number-name
Extract epic_num, story_num, story_key, story_title from found entry
From {epics_content}, extract Epic {{epic_num}} context:
Extract story ({{epic_num}}-{{story_num}}) details: user story, acceptance criteria, technical requirements

check.action: Extract dev notes, learnings, patterns established
Get recent commits for work patterns and conventions

step.action: Load and analyze architecture content for story-relevant requirements
Extract: technical stack, code structure, API patterns, security requirements, testing standards

step.goal: Web research for latest technical specifics
step.action: Research latest versions of libraries/frameworks identified in architecture

step.critical: 📝 CREATE STORY FILE - Developer's implementation guide
step.action: Initialize from template.md to {default_output_file}
Set story Status to: "ready-for-dev"
Parse the Tasks/Subtasks section from the created story file
For each task, extract: - Task index (1, 2, 3...) - Task description - AC reference (if present) - Dependencies (parse "(depends: task-N)" from description)
Store as {{tasks_list}} for td creation
Set {{td_task_count}} = length of tasks_list
Construct epic title: "Story {{epic_num}}.{{story_num}}: {{story_title}}"
Construct epic body with: - User story statement - Acceptance criteria summary - Link to story file path
Parse response to extract {{td_epic_id}}
Set {{sync_timestamp}} = current timestamp

step.goal: Create td issues for each task
step.action: Initialize {{td_task_mapping}} = empty list
Initialize {{dependency_registry}} = empty dict

iterate.action: Extract task description and any dependency hints

check.action: Resolve dependency to td_issue_id from dependency_registry

iterate.command:
td add "{{task.description}}" \ --parent {{td_epic_id}} \ --tag bmad --tag task \ --body "**AC Reference:** {{task.ac_reference}}
📁 Story File: \`{{default_output_file}}\`" \ --metadata story_file="{{default_output_file}}" \ --metadata task_index="{{i}}" \ {{depends_on_flag}}

step.goal: Update story file with td references
step.action: Add "## td Integration" section to story file with: - td Epic ID - Task count summary - Sync timestamp - Task → td mapping table
Update each task line to include td reference: "- [ ] Task 1 (AC: #1) [td:td-abc1]"
Add td Sync Log entry: "{{sync_timestamp}} | initialized | Story created with td epic {{td_epic_id}}"
Save story file

step.output:
**🎯 BMAD-TD STORY CREATED, {{user_name}}!**
**Story Details:** - Story ID: {{epic_num}}.{{story_num}} - Story Key: {{story_key}} - File: {{default_output_file}} - Status: ready-for-dev
**td Integration:** - td Epic: {{td_epic_id}} - td Tasks: {{td_task_count}} issues created - Sync Status: initialized
**Next Steps:** 1. Review the story file: {{default_output_file}} 2. Run `/bmad:td:dev-next` to start the first task (Ralph Wiggum loop) 3. Each task runs in its own session with td handoff/review flow 4. After all tasks complete, run `/bmad:td:review-story` for epic-level review
**Key Benefits:** - 📊 Full observability via sidecar - 🔄 Automatic status sync between story file and td - 🎯 Ralph Wiggum loop: one bite-sized chunk per session - ✅ Separate review sessions enforced by td
